2605.05738.title: CoMemNet: Contrastive Sampling with Memory Replay Network for Continual Traffic Prediction
2605.05738.paper_summary: In recent years, the integration of non-topological space modeling with temporal learning methods has emerged as an effective approach for capturing spatio-temporal information in non-Euclidean graphs. However, most existing methods rely on static underlying graph structures, which are inadequate for capturing the continuously expanding and evolving patterns in streaming traffic networks. To address this challenge, we propose a simple yet efficient dual-branch continual learning framework for traffic prediction, named CoMemNet. The fast-converging Online branch undertakes the primary prediction tasks, while the momentum-updated Target branch extracts historical information using Wasserstein Distance features to create a Dynamic Contrastive Sampler (DC Sampler). This sampler selects a node set with significant dynamic network feature changes for training, effectively mitigating the issue of catastrophic forgetting. Additionally, the backbone incorporates a lightweight Node-Adaptive Temporal Memory Buffer (TMRB-N) to consolidate old knowledge through memory replay and address the risk of memory explosion. Finally, we provide two newly curated open-source datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that CoMemNet achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance across all three large-scale real-world datasets. The code is available at: https://github.com/meiwu5/CoMemNet.
2605.05738.paper_summary_zh: 近年來，非拓撲空間建模與時間學習方法的整合已成為捕捉非歐幾里得圖中時空信息的有效方法。<lb/>然而，大多數現有方法依賴於靜態的基礎圖結構，這對於捕捉流量網絡中不斷擴展和演變的模式來說是不足夠的。<lb/>為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了一個簡單而高效的雙分支持續學習框架，用於流量預測，名為 CoMemNet。<lb/>快速收斂的在線分支承擔主要的預測任務，而動量更新的目標分支則利用 Wasserstein 距離特徵提取歷史信息，以創建動態對比取樣器（DC Sampler）。<lb/>這個取樣器選擇一組在動態網絡特徵變化上顯著的節點集進行訓練，有效地減輕了災難性遺忘的問題。<lb/>此外，主幹還包含一個輕量級的節點自適應時間記憶緩衝區（TMRB-N），通過記憶重播來鞏固舊知識，並解決記憶爆炸的風險。<lb/>最後，我們提供了兩個新編輯的開源數據集。<lb/>實驗結果表明，CoMemNet 在所有三個大型現實世界數據集中達到了最先進的（SOTA）性能。<lb/>代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/meiwu5/CoMemNet。
2605.05738.author: Mei Wu et.al.
2605.05738.authors: Mei Wu, Wenchao Weng, Wenxin Su, Wenjie Tang, Wei Zhou
